Geography Lesson Plan
Subject: Geography
Topic: Population Distribution in Nigeria
Class: SSS1 | Duration: 40 minutes | Board: NERDC

Objective: By the end of this lesson, students will be able to:
1. Apply the population density formula to calculate densities for different states
2. Use a population map to identify high- and low-density zones
3. Explain push and pull factors of migration using a case study

Materials: NERDC Geography textbook SSS1, population maps, calculators, whiteboard.

Introduction (5 min):
Teacher John asks: 'How many of you have moved from a smaller town to this city?' Hands go up. Mary explains her family moved from the countryside. The class discusses why people move.

Main Activity (25 min):
1. Teacher introduces population density formula: Density = Population ÷ Area (km²).
2. Students calculate densities: Lagos, Kano, Benue.
3. Peter and James use the population map to identify patterns.
4. Class discusses push-pull theory: Michael explains push factors, Elizabeth explains pull factors.
5. Group activity: solve a real problem about population data.

Assessment (10 min):
Students apply the formula and justify which state faces greater urban pressure.

Closure:
Recap formula. Homework: Calculate density for home state.
reference: NERDC SSS1 Geography lesson on population distribution in Nigeria covering density calculations, mapping, and push-pull migration factors with Nigerian names and data.
subject: geography
notes: Documented pre-revision state from production system: Western names used throughout (John, Mary, Peter, James, Michael, Elizabeth) — no West African names. cultural_flag_check rule triggered revision.
